rationale: Long-only equity base-breakout strategy with a structured must-have/would-like-to-have/cannot-have filter system. Rising 20MA under price, tight consolidation base, high-quality overnight gap on daily for directional bias, minimum 2:1 R:R, and a fixed entry-over-base/stop-under-base mechanical structure. Position sizing is formulaic. All conditions are observable from daily OHLCV and gap data, making this testable despite some missing quantitative thresholds.

claims.summary: Rising 20MA under price with a tight consolidation base (narrow range bars, clean, minimal tails). Entry triggered when price breaks above the base high. Pre-entry screening eliminates sloppy bases, time-frame conflicts, spready/low-liquidity stocks, and setups unable to reach 2:1 R:R.
claims.trigger_rule: Rising 20-period moving average trading under price and trending higher; a tight consolidation base with narrow range bars and few tails; entry taken over the base high
claims.invalidation_rule: Sloppy base with tails throughout; time-frame conflict across daily/hourly/15m/5m; spready conditions or average daily volume below a few million shares; less than 2:1 risk-to-reward potential
missing_fields: Quantitative tight-base definition (bar count, range percentage)
Consecutive bar count for 'rising' MA confirmation

claims.summary: A high-quality overnight gap on the daily time frame establishes the larger-term bullish bias. The trader checks the gap on the daily chart first, then drops to smaller time frames to find the entry setup.
claims.trigger_rule: High-quality overnight gap present on the daily time frame, providing bullish bias before checking lower time frames for a base-breakout entry
required_data: daily OHLCV including gap data (open vs previous close)
missing_fields: Quantitative criteria for 'high-quality' gap (gap percentage, volume confirmation, gap type)
Minimum gap age or fill condition
Decision rule if gap is absent

claims.summary: Fixed exit structure: entry is over the base high, stop-loss is under the base low. Minimum profit target is 2:1 risk-to-reward. Position sizing is share size = risk / (entry price minus stop-loss price), where risk is the dollar amount the trader is psychologically comfortable losing per trade.
claims.trigger_rule: Entry triggered at price above the base high; stop-loss placed below the base low
claims.invalidation_rule: Stop-loss triggers at the base low; trade skipped if setup cannot achieve minimum 2:1 R:R
missing_fields: Specific profit-target method (fixed R:R multiple, trailing stop, or partial scale-out)
Trailing stop or adjustment rules if price moves favorably
Maximum per-day loss rule (mentioned as concept but no specific threshold)

candidate.title: Multi-Filter Base Breakout with Daily Gap Bias
candidate.summary: A long-only swing strategy that buys breakouts above a tight consolidation base when: (1) the 20MA is rising under price, (2) a high-quality overnight gap on the daily provides bullish bias, (3) volume was low during the base with a spike on breakout, (4) multiple time frames are aligned, and (5) the setup has at least 2:1 R:R. The stop is fixed under the base low, and position size is risk / (entry - stop). The hypothesis is that this multi-factor filter system generates a positive expectancy on US equities without needing intraday Level 2 data.
candidate.data_requirements: Daily and intraday OHLCV (hourly, 15m, 5m) for US equities with average daily volume >1M shares. Gap data computable from daily open vs previous close. Backtesting window of at least 3 years.
candidate.prior_art: Base-breakout with rising MA is a well-known pattern across many trading methodologies (Minervini VCP, Darvas box, cup-and-handle). The specific must-have filter list plus overnight gap bias adds structure but is not novel in isolation.
candidate.structural_difference: Not conclusively established from the source alone. The multi-layer filter checklist (must-have / would-like-to-have / cannot-have) with a fixed over-base entry and under-base stop is more systematic than discretionary breakout trading, but shares the same underlying pattern as many published breakout strategies.
assumption_register: Tight base definition: freeze the bar count and range-percentage threshold for consolidation
Rising 20MA: freeze the slope period and minimum value
High-quality overnight gap: freeze gap percentage minimum, direction, and age
Volume conditions: freeze the low-volume base threshold and breakout-volume spike multiple
Shakeout candle: freeze the definition (bottoming tail, body range, position within base)
Time frame alignment: freeze which time frames and the alignment criteria
Profit target: freeze target method (fixed R:R or trailing rule)
Entry trigger: freeze bar-over-base-high or intraday-breakout threshold
